derivatives.name: hy_ig_spread_pct
derivatives.definition: The raw HY-IG credit spread — the extra yield investors demand to hold high-yield bonds relative to investment-grade bonds, expressed in percentage points. A wider spread signals credit stress.
derivatives.formula: raw column (ICE BofA US HY OAS minus ICE BofA US IG OAS), unit = percent
derivatives.notes: Canonical indicator column after Wave 5C DATA-D12 rename (was `hy_ig_spread`). Sign convention: up = wider = more stress. Daily 2000-01-03 to 2025-12-31. Monthly-release OAS series aligned to daily NYSE calendar via LVCF.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_zscore_252d
derivatives.definition: The 252-day (1-year) rolling z-score of the HY-IG spread. It tells you how unusual today's spread is relative to the last twelve months.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S2a. Used as threshold input for T3_z1.5 / T3_z2.0 / T3_z2.5 gates. Dimensionless.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_zscore_504d
derivatives.definition: The 504-day (~2-year) rolling z-score of the HY-IG spread. A longer lookback than the 252d variant to capture slower-moving regime shifts.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S2b. Longer window to reduce sensitivity to near-term mean drift.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_pctrank_504d
derivatives.definition: The 2-year percentile rank of the HY-IG spread. A value of 0.90 means the current spread is wider than 90% of the last two years.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S3a. Values in [0, 1]. Used with T1_p75/T1_p85/T1_p95 threshold variants.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_pctrank_1260d
derivatives.definition: The 5-year percentile rank of the HY-IG spread. A longer-memory gauge of how extreme the current spread is relative to recent cycle history.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S3b. Values in [0, 1].

derivatives.name: hy_ig_roc_21d
derivatives.definition: The 21-day (~1 month) percent rate-of-change of the HY-IG spread. Captures near-term momentum — is the spread widening or tightening over the last month?
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S4a. Unit = percent. Monthly momentum diagnostic.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_roc_63d
derivatives.definition: The 63-day (~1 quarter) percent rate-of-change of the HY-IG spread. Captures quarterly widening or tightening momentum.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S4b. Quarterly momentum. Unit = percent.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_roc_126d
derivatives.definition: The 126-day (~6 months) percent rate-of-change of the HY-IG spread. Semi-annual momentum gauge.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S4c. Unit = percent.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_mom_21d
derivatives.definition: The 21-day absolute change (in percentage points) of the HY-IG spread. An absolute-difference twin of the 21d rate-of-change, expressed in the spread's native unit.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S10. Unit = percentage points (matches the parent spread).

derivatives.name: hy_ig_mom_63d
derivatives.definition: The 63-day absolute change (in percentage points) of the HY-IG spread — captures one quarter of widening or tightening in the spread's native unit.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S11. Strongest raw-correlation HY-IG derivative at 63d (r = -0.17).

derivatives.name: hy_ig_mom_252d
derivatives.definition: The 252-day (~1 year) absolute change in the HY-IG spread, in percentage points. A long-horizon momentum gauge useful for cycle-scale analysis.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S12.

derivatives.name: hy_ig_acceleration
derivatives.definition: The change in the HY-IG spread's 21-day rate-of-change — a second-difference proxy. Rising acceleration means the spread is widening faster than it was a month ago.
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S13. Captures inflection dynamics (the derivative of momentum).

derivatives.name: hy_ig_realized_vol_21d
derivatives.definition: The 21-day annualized realized volatility of daily HY-IG spread changes. Captures how choppy the spread has been recently, irrespective of direction.
derivatives.notes: Decimal output (0.30 = 30% annualized). Exploratory diagnostic — not shipped as a tournament signal in Wave 2; present in exploratory correlations.csv.

derivatives.name: hmm_2state_prob_stress
derivatives.definition: The probability that the HY-IG spread is currently in the high-stress regime according to a 2-state Gaussian Hidden Markov Model. Values near 1 mean the model is confident we are in a stressed regime.
derivatives.formula: posterior P(regime = stress | hy_ig_spread_pct_{1..t}) from 2-state Gaussian HMM
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S6. WINNER signal (OOS Sharpe 1.274 vs B&H 0.77). HMM fitted on the HY-IG spread series alone; stress regime identified by higher-variance cluster per Defense 1 column-name discipline.

derivatives.name: hmm_2state_prob_calm
derivatives.definition: The mirror of the stress probability — how likely the model thinks we are in the calm, low-variance regime.
derivatives.notes: Complement of hmm_2state_prob_stress by construction. Rendered as the lower area in the regime-probability stacked chart.

derivatives.name: hmm_2state_state
derivatives.definition: The discrete regime label from the 2-state HMM (0 = calm, 1 = stress) — the hard-classified version of the stress probability.
derivatives.formula: argmax over states of the HMM posterior at time t
derivatives.notes: Diagnostic-only hard label; production charts use the probability, not the state label, to avoid flicker. Present in core_models_20260410/hmm_states_2state.parquet.

derivatives.name: ms_2state_stress_prob
derivatives.definition: The 2-state Markov-switching regression's estimate of the probability we are in the high-stress regime — an alternative to the HMM model with similar economic interpretation.
derivatives.formula: smoothed P(regime = stress | hy_ig_spread_pct_{1..T}) from a 2-regime Markov-switching mean-variance model
derivatives.notes: Tournament signal code S7. Cross-check against HMM; correlated but not identical. Used with T5_ms_0.5 / T5_ms_0.7 thresholds.

derivatives.name: spy
derivatives.definition: The raw adjusted close price of the SPY ETF (SPDR S&P 500). All target-axis derivatives are computed from this single series.
derivatives.formula: raw column (Yahoo Finance adjusted close, USD)
derivatives.notes: Adjusted for dividends and splits. Daily series 2000-01-03 to 2025-12-31.

derivatives.name: spy_ret
derivatives.definition: The 1-day simple return of SPY. Base input to most Strategy-page calculations (equity curves, Sharpe, volatility).
derivatives.notes: Decimal return (0.01 = 1%).

derivatives.name: spy_fwd_1d
derivatives.definition: The forward 1-day return of SPY — what SPY returns between today's close and tomorrow's close. Used for short-horizon correlation diagnostics.
derivatives.notes: Shortest horizon in the correlation heatmap. Forward-looking — leakage-safe by construction.

derivatives.name: spy_fwd_5d
derivatives.definition: The forward 5-trading-day (~1 week) return of SPY. Short-horizon predictive response.
derivatives.notes: Weekly horizon diagnostic.

derivatives.name: spy_fwd_21d
derivatives.definition: The forward 21-trading-day (~1 month) return of SPY. Monthly horizon used across correlation and CCF analyses.
derivatives.notes: Common evaluation horizon for cross-correlation and Granger diagnostics.

derivatives.name: spy_fwd_63d
derivatives.definition: The forward 63-trading-day (~1 quarter) return of SPY. Primary dependent variable for predictive regressions on this pair.
derivatives.notes: Primary forecast horizon; aligns with tournament holding-period bias.

derivatives.name: spy_fwd_126d
derivatives.definition: The forward 126-trading-day (~6 months) return of SPY. Medium-term horizon used in correlation matrix and horizon-decay checks.
derivatives.notes: Semi-annual diagnostic.

derivatives.name: spy_fwd_252d
derivatives.definition: The forward 252-trading-day (~1 year) return of SPY. Longest horizon in the correlation heatmap; tests whether signals retain predictive value over annual windows.
derivatives.notes: Annual horizon.

derivatives.name: spy_drawdown_path
derivatives.definition: The running drawdown of SPY from its trailing all-time high. Used on the Strategy page to show how the winning strategy behaves during SPY drawdowns.
derivatives.notes: Non-positive by construction. Companion to equity_curves on the Strategy page.

derivatives.name: spy_equity_curve
derivatives.definition: The cumulative total-return index of SPY — a $1 starting equity grown by the 1-day SPY returns. Used as the benchmark line on the Strategy-page equity-curve chart.
derivatives.notes: Buy-and-hold benchmark trace; the strategy equity curves are layered on top.

notes: Wave 7B retro-apply: populates the META-CF ECON-SD / ECON-UD scope registry for HY-IG v2 after Lesandro's Wave 6 scope-discipline feedback flagged 5 off-scope traces in the correlation heatmap. Canonical indicator column = hy_ig_spread_pct (Dana's Wave 5C DATA-D12 rename); canonical target column = spy. Off-scope signals (NFCI Momentum, Bank/Smallcap Ratio, Yield Curve 10y-3m, BBB-IG Spread, CCC-BB Spread) are explicitly NOT included here per ECON-SD — they are logged as informational suggestions in analyst_suggestions.json per ECON-AS. Vera's Wave 7B chart regeneration is the downstream consumer that will drop those 5 traces from the heatmap.
